exploration_log.aha_moment: The old middleware loop iterated allMethods and only set the header when OPTIONS was found in the list. Removing that gate and directly setting the header (when len>0) plus adding OPTIONS unconditionally in getAllMethodsForRoute is the fix.
exploration_log.hindsight: The test 'does not set without OPTIONS matcher' documented the bug, not correct behavior. Updating the test name and expected value documents the new intended behavior.

alternatives: Add OPTIONS only in the middleware loop, not in getAllMethodsForRoute
Add OPTIONS unconditionally in getAllMethodsForRoute even when no routes match
key_decisions.decision: Add OPTIONS in getAllMethodsForRoute only when len(allMethods) > 0 and remove the OPTIONS gate in the middleware
key_decisions.reasoning: Keeps the concern in getAllMethodsForRoute, avoids adding OPTIONS when no routes match the path, and makes the middleware simpler (no loop)

solve_notes: Two changes in middleware.go: (1) In CORSMethodMiddleware, removed the inner loop that checked for OPTIONS before setting the header — now sets the header whenever allMethods is non-empty. (2) In getAllMethodsForRoute, added an unconditional OPTIONS append (using matchInArray to avoid duplication) when allMethods is non-empty. Also updated the corresponding test from asserting empty header to asserting GET,PUT,PATCH,OPTIONS.
task_text:
Fix CORSMethodMiddleware not including OPTIONS in the Access-Control-Allow-Methods header

`CORSMethodMiddleware` sets the `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` header
based on methods registered for a route, but it only sets the header
when `OPTIONS` is already present in the collected methods list. When a
route handles OPTIONS explicitly (e.g., via `Methods(http.MethodOptions,
...)`), OPTIONS is included; however, the collected methods may omit
OPTIONS for routes that rely on the CORS middleware itself to handle
preflight. Fix `getAllMethodsForRoute` and the middleware loop in
`middleware.go` to always append `OPTIONS` to the methods list when
building the `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` response header, ensuring
preflight responses are correct even when OPTIONS is not in a route's
explicit method matcher.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: directly tests CORSMethodMiddleware and getAllMethodsForRoute for all scenarios including the one testing the OPTIONS-gate behavior being changed
tests CORSMethodMiddleware on a subrouter, exercising getAllMethodsForRoute with subrouter routes

thrash_preventing_defs.name: matchInArray
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to know this utility exists before writing the OPTIONS dedup check; prevents an agent from writing its own loop

thrash_preventing_defs.name: GetMethods
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding what GetMethods() returns (the registered method matchers) confirms why OPTIONS won't be in the list unless explicitly registered

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the two directly edited functions. The thrash_preventing set adds matchInArray (so an agent uses the existing utility for OPTIONS dedup instead of writing a new loop) and GetMethods (so an agent understands why OPTIONS is absent from the collected list). Without these, an agent might reinvent the array-search logic or not understand the root cause.
